task:
Workspace-segmentation atom: READ-CONSUMER ALIGNMENT for workspace-local run display numbers. Run_137 landed the portable-history WRITE side (per ADR-0027 and cocoder/priorities/workspace-segmentation.owner-map.md rows 6 and 8): each run's durable record lives at `<workspace>/cocoder/runs/<displayNumber>-<runId>/run.json` carrying `run.displayNumber`, written via packages/core/src/store/portable/. DO NOT redo or modify the write side, the portable file format, the counters, or the migration. The remaining gap: the daemon read routes and the UI still surface the GLOBAL internal `run.id`, never the workspace-local `displayNumber`. Close that.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a portable read helper (in packages/core/src/store/portable/, exported via index.ts) that resolves a run's portable record (and thus its `displayNumber`) given only (primaryRoot, runId) — i.e. discover the `<displayNumber>-<runId>` directory by its `-<runId>` suffix under `<primaryRoot>/cocoder/runs/`, then read run.json. Reuse existing helpers in runs.ts/paths.ts (readPortableRun, portableRunPaths, portableRunDirName); do not duplicate path logic.
2. Daemon `GET /runs` (listRuns, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts ~line 409) and `GET /runs/:id` (runDetail, ~line 415): for each run, resolve the workspace primary root from the registry (runDetail already calls findWorkspace; listRuns must resolve the workspace path from its workspaceId filter / each run's workspaceId) and include the portable `displayNumber` on the surfaced run object. The portable run.json is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for displayNumber; the SQLite DB stays the rebuildable machine-local index (ADR-0027) — you may keep listing/looking up runs via ctx.store, but the displayNumber must come from the portable file, not re-derived from run.id.
3. UI: render the workspace-local number (e.g. `Run 1`) instead of the raw global `run.id` wherever the run identity is shown to the user — run summary list and run detail (packages/ui/app/live.ts adaptRuns/adaptRunDetail and packages/ui/app/adapter.ts, packages/ui/app/App.tsx run rendering). Keep the internal run.id available for deep-links/addressing (it must stay the key for /runs/:id, teardown, deep links) — only the HUMAN-FACING label changes to the workspace-local number.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `GET /runs?workspace=cocoder` and `GET /runs/:id` responses include a `displayNumber` sourced from the portable run.json.
- The dashboard shows the workspace-local run number, not the global run.id, for run identity.
- Add/extend tests proving the read path surfaces the portable displayNumber (a core test for the new portable read helper, and a daemon route test asserting displayNumber appears in /runs and /runs/:id output). Reuse existing test fixtures/harnesses for the store and routes.

MUST NOT BREAK (graceful degradation is required):
- A run with NO portable run.json (legacy DB-only runs from before the run_137 dual-write, or the unexecuted backfill migration) must NOT crash the read route — fall back to showing the global run.id (or null displayNumber the UI tolerates), never throw. Add a test for the missing-portable-file case.
- run.id stays the addressing/deep-link/teardown key everywhere — do not swap the route param, store key, liveRefs, or session refs to displayNumber.
- Do not change the portable write side, counters.json, the migration, or the portable file schema. Do not touch unrelated UI behavior (cards/modals/tickets) beyond the run-number label.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: the diff; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (or the repo's core test command) and the daemon test command green; typecheck green; and the exact test names you added for (a) the portable read helper, (b) the route surfacing displayNumber, (c) the missing-portable-file fallback. Write scope: packages/core/src/store/portable/**, packages/core/src/store/portable/index.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/ui/app/** (run-number rendering only), and the corresponding test files under packages/core/tests/** and packages/daemon/tests/**.

Workspace-segmentation atom: PROVE-AND-HARDEN concurrent workspace sessions (Objective 6 — "different workspaces cannot collide"). The owner map's Collision / Concurrency Assumptions table (cocoder/priorities/workspace-segmentation.owner-map.md) is your checklist. Two runs in two DIFFERENT workspaces operate on different codebases and must be able to run at the same time without colliding. Establish this BY EVIDENCE: for each shared resource, determine whether it is already safe-by-design or a real collision bug, then fix the real bugs and prove isolation with a test.

AUDIT (each row of the collision table — report a one-line verdict per item: SAFE-BY-DESIGN with the proof, or BUG-FIXED with the change):
1. Install-wide SQLite DB + writer lock (`packages/core/src/store/sqlite-store.ts`, opened in `packages/daemon/src/server.ts`): can two concurrent runs in different workspaces both write (createRun/createSession/recordEvent/setRunStatus) without a 'database is locked'/SQLITE_BUSY failure? If node:sqlite serializes safely, prove it; if concurrent writes can throw, add the minimal guard (e.g. busy handling/retry) — do NOT split into per-workspace DBs (Fixed Decision: one shared local/ zone).
2. Launch lock `ctx.inFlight` (`packages/daemon/src/context.ts`, reserve/release in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`): confirm it is keyed PER workspaceId so a run in workspace A does not block a launch in workspace B. If any launch/reserve path is keyed globally instead of per-workspace, fix it. Show the keying.
3. Run-dir root `local/runs/<runId>` (`packages/daemon/src/server.ts` runsRoot, `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`, `packages/daemon/src/rundir.ts`) and `resumeFromRunId`: confirm globally-unique run IDs (Fixed Decision allows this) mean no on-disk collision across workspaces. State the evidence; only change something if a real collision exists.
4. Single daemon SSE stream (`/oz/events`, `packages/daemon/src/context.ts` event bus, `packages/ui/electron/events-stream.ts`): confirm cross-workspace events are correctly attributed by `workspaceId` and a workspace-B event cannot corrupt workspace-A state in a concurrent session. If events lacking a workspaceId can mis-route, note it; fix only if it is a real correctness bug (not cosmetic).
5. Portable history + counters (`<workspace>/cocoder/runs/`, `cocoder/counters.json`): confirm these are already per-workspace files so concurrent runs in different workspaces write to different trees (no shared counter/file). Show the path separation.
6. Git dirty-tree / commit guards (`packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts` direct-mode preflight, `packages/core/src/commit-gate/`): confirm they operate on each workspace's own repo path so two repos' working trees are independent. State the evidence.

DELIVERABLE (the spine — this is the exit criterion):
- Add a regression test that exercises TWO concurrent runs in TWO different workspaces and asserts isolation across the audited resources: separate run directories, separate portable run.json/history trees, independent inFlight locks (A running does not block B), both runs' DB writes succeed, and events are attributed to the correct workspace. Put it in the package where the harness fits best (likely packages/daemon/tests/ for end-to-end launch isolation, or packages/core/tests/ for store/runner-level concurrency). Reuse the existing store/server test harnesses (see packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts and packages/core/tests/store.test.ts patterns); do not stand up new infra if an existing harness fits.
- Fix every REAL collision the audit/test surfaces (minimal, root-cause). If a fix turns out to be large or risky beyond this atom's scope, STOP and report it as a blocker rather than half-implementing — name the exact resource and the required change.

MUST NOT BREAK:
- Do not change internal run IDs to per-workspace numbering (Fixed Decision: globally-unique internal IDs stay). Do not create per-workspace machine-local folders inside a repo. Do not split the shared SQLite DB. Do not touch the portable write side / file format / counters semantics landed in run_137. Do not regress single-workspace behavior — the existing single-run-per-workspace lock and current tests must stay green.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: the diff; the per-resource audit verdicts (1–6 above, SAFE-BY-DESIGN-with-proof or BUG-FIXED-with-change); the new concurrency test name; and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, and `pnpm typecheck` all green. Write scope: packages/core/src/store/**, packages/core/src/runner/**, packages/daemon/src/context.ts, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/src/server.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts (only if a real collision requires it), and the corresponding test files under packages/core/tests/** and packages/daemon/tests/**. Stay out of the UI for this atom unless a real event-mis-routing bug requires a renderer fix.

Workspace-segmentation atom: make the portable-history BACKFILL migration RUNNABLE and IDEMPOTENT (Objective 4 — durable history travels with the repo, including runs that predate the run_137 dual-write). Run_137 landed `migrateWorkspacePortableHistory` in packages/core/src/store/portable/migrate.ts but it has NO caller (dead code) and a real correctness hazard: it numbers runs positionally (`index + 1`) and blindly overwrites cocoder/counters.json, so on a repo that already has SOME portable history from the live dual-write (e.g. `1-run_138` exists now) it would renumber/orphan existing portable run dirs. Fix both.

USE CASE TO DESIGN FOR: the migration runs on the machine that HAS the SQLite DB (local/cocoder.db), to materialize portable history under the workspace's tracked cocoder/ tree for any runs not yet portable, so the repo carries full history when it moves to another machine (where the DB is empty/machine-local). It will routinely encounter a repo that already has partial portable history from the dual-write — so it MUST reconcile, not clobber.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Harden `migrateWorkspacePortableHistory` (packages/core/src/store/portable/migrate.ts; touch projection.ts/counters.ts only if required) to be IDEMPOTENT and RECONCILING: discover which runs already have a portable run dir (reuse the run_137/atom-0 helpers — `readPortableRunById` / list `portableWorkspacePaths(primaryRoot).runsDir`), KEEP their existing displayNumber (never renumber or orphan an existing portable run), and only materialize portable history for runs missing it, assigning new display numbers ABOVE the current max. Recompute counters.json correctly: nextRunDisplayNumber = max(existing portable display numbers, newly assigned) + 1; nextSessionDisplayNumber and nextTicketNumber computed consistently rather than blindly = count+1. Running the migration TWICE in a row must be a no-op (no duplicate dirs, no changed numbers, no counter drift).
2. Wire ONE runnable entrypoint following the existing `cocoder oz <subcommand>` → daemon-route pattern (mirror teardown / commit-support): add a daemon route (e.g. POST /workspaces/:id/migrate-portable-history in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts) that resolves the workspace from the registry (findWorkspace) and calls the migration with ctx.store, returning { runsExported, sessionsExported }; add a `cocoder oz migrate-history <workspaceId>` subcommand in packages/cli/src/run.ts plus the client call in packages/cli/src/client.ts, and print the returned counts. Match the surrounding usage/error-message style.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (tests are the exit criterion):
- Core test: (a) fresh backfill on a DB-only workspace materializes every run's portable run.json + sessions/work-items/commits/events history with correct, createdAt-ordered display numbers and correct counters.json; (b) running it a SECOND time is a no-op (identical tree, unchanged numbers/counters); (c) PARTIAL state — a workspace where one run already has portable history — fills only the gaps and PRESERVES the existing run's displayNumber.
- Daemon (or CLI) test: the route/command invokes the migration and returns/prints runsExported/sessionsExported. Reuse existing store/server harnesses (packages/core/tests/store.test.ts, packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts patterns).

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
- DO NOT execute the migration on this live repo — only wire + test it. Materializing tracked history on the real repo is a founder/operational decision, not part of this atom.
- Do not change the dual-write side, the portable file format, or counters semantics landed in run_137. Do not renumber or move any existing portable run dir. Do not change internal run IDs. Keep all existing tests green.
- If the idempotency redesign turns out larger or riskier than this atom (e.g. requires changing projection.ts contracts broadly), STOP and report the exact blocker rather than half-implementing.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: the diff; the three new core test names + the route/CLI test name; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, and `pnpm typecheck` all green; and the `cocoder oz migrate-history` usage line. Write scope: packages/core/src/store/portable/migrate.ts, packages/core/src/store/portable/projection.ts, packages/core/src/store/portable/counters.ts, packages/core/src/store/portable/index.ts, packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/cli/src/run.ts, packages/cli/src/client.ts, and test files under packages/core/tests/** and packages/daemon/tests/**.

Workspace-segmentation atom (PROOF LAST): build a one-command proof harness that demonstrates the priority's machine-checkable 'Verified when' objectives end-to-end, so the founder can run ONE command and see the workspace boundary proven. The feature code is complete and unit/integration-tested across run_135–138; this atom does NOT add feature code — it makes the objective-level verification runnable, following this repo's existing proof-script convention (`scripts/proof-priorities-queue.mjs` + the `proof:queue` script in package.json — read it first and MATCH its shape, output style, and exit-code discipline).

CREATE `scripts/proof-workspace-segmentation.mjs` and add a `proof:workspace-segmentation` entry to the root package.json scripts. The harness must set up a throwaway temp install/workspaces (never touch the real repo's tracked cocoder/ tree or local/), drive the REAL core/portable/runner APIs and the daemon read routes (reuse the actual exported functions — do not reimplement logic or copy test internals), and assert + print a human-readable OBJECTIVE → EVIDENCE map. Exit non-zero if any machine-checkable assertion fails.

MACHINE-CHECKABLE OBJECTIVES TO PROVE (map each to a printed PASS line with the concrete evidence):
- Obj 3 + 8 (workspace-local state, named owners): two workspaces each get independent workspace-local run display numbers from their OWN cocoder/counters.json + portable run.json (both start at 1 independently); priorities/tickets/counters live under each workspace's tracked cocoder/ tree, not shared.
- Obj 4 (durable history travels + runnable backfill): a run's durable record is written under <workspace>/cocoder/runs/<n>-<runId>/ (run.json + sessions/work-items/commits/events), is readable back via the portable read API, and `migrateWorkspacePortableHistory` backfills a DB-only workspace and is idempotent on a second run (runsExported 0, tree unchanged).
- Obj 5 (DB classified): the portable files are the source of truth for durable history while local/cocoder.db is the rebuildable index — assert the portable run.json round-trips the run's identity/target/status independently of the DB.
- Obj 6 (concurrent by construction): two runs in two different workspaces hold independent in-flight locks and write to separate run dirs + separate portable trees with NO cross-contamination (reuse the same isolation assertions proven in the atom-1 daemon test, but at the proof-harness level).
- Obj 7 (labels): the cmux group label for a run includes workspace identity + launched target type + target slug + run identity — assert the real `groupLabel()` output contains all four for a sample run.

NON-MACHINE-CHECKABLE OBJECTIVES (Obj 1 visual Oz/workspace split + ~50% wider panel + Oz controls in the terminal panel; Obj 2 chat target picker incl. global Oz) — do NOT fake these. Print them explicitly as FOUNDER-EYEBALL checks with exactly where to look in the running app (the dashboard section/component), so the harness output is an honest, complete objective map rather than silently omitting them. Note in the output that they are covered by the run_136 UI tests (cite the test files) for the machine-checkable portion.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `pnpm proof:workspace-segmentation` (i.e. `node scripts/proof-workspace-segmentation.mjs`) runs to completion, prints the objective→evidence map, and exits 0 when everything passes; exits non-zero if any machine-checkable assertion fails (demonstrate the failure path is real, e.g. by construction, not a hardcoded pass).
- The harness uses only temp dirs and cleans them up; running it leaves the real repo's git status unchanged (verify `git status` is clean of stray files after a run).
- It reuses real exported APIs from @cocoder/core and the daemon; it does not duplicate migration/portable/label logic.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
- No feature-code changes — scripts/ and package.json only (plus, if strictly needed, a tiny exported helper, but prefer using what's already exported). Do not touch the real cocoder/ tree or local/. Do not weaken or duplicate existing tests. Keep all suites + typecheck green.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: the diff; the FULL stdout of `pnpm proof:workspace-segmentation` (the objective→evidence map, exit 0); confirmation `git status` is clean of stray temp artifacts afterward; and `pnpm typecheck` green. Write scope: scripts/proof-workspace-segmentation.mjs and the root package.json scripts block.
